canonical_reference.rule: A release crossmatch can bind an identifier to an existing permanent object, but cannot create, merge, split, or reparent canonical objects in E2.

gaia_release_reconciliation.acceptance: exactly one DR3 candidate for the targeted DR2 identifier and exactly one DR2 predecessor for that DR3 identifier in the independently acquired reverse neighborhood
gaia_release_reconciliation.missing: the targeted DR2 identifier has no official DR3 neighborhood row
gaia_release_reconciliation.ambiguous_split: the targeted DR2 identifier has more than one official DR3 candidate
gaia_release_reconciliation.ambiguous_merge: the sole DR3 candidate has more than one official DR2 predecessor
gaia_release_reconciliation.excluded: the release mapping is accepted but the DR3 identifier is absent from the current canonical stability reference
gaia_release_reconciliation.quarantined: reverse evidence is incomplete, official pair payloads conflict, or the DR3 identifier binds to multiple canonical objects
gaia_release_reconciliation.proper_motion_rule: The official proper_motion_propagation flag records whether epoch propagation was used; it is a safeguard signal, not evidence that the source has high proper motion. High proper motion is reported separately from accepted canonical vector motion.
gaia_release_reconciliation.identifier_rule: Gaia DR2 and Gaia DR3 source IDs are release-scoped values and are never compared as interchangeable identifiers.

scope_kinds.physical_identity: A source record purports to describe the same physical object as an identifier node.
scope_kinds.system_containment: A source explicitly claims parent/child physical containment; candidate relations are never promoted automatically.
scope_kinds.component_or_subsystem: A source record is scoped to a named component, unresolved component, pair, or subsystem.
scope_kinds.observation_target: An observation or target-list row points at an identifier without establishing physical identity or containment.
scope_kinds.alias_or_public_name: A name or alias labels an object or target and remains independent from physical identity.
